title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™
notes:
Can't connect to the internet on launch, so it launches a web browser with a code copy mechanism to verify ownership.

After this process, game crashes with the following error message.

"The Windows Local Application Data Folder is inaccessible. Access to this folder is required.

*USERDIR*\WB Games\Shadow of War\"

Manually creating this folder does not resolve the issue.
Won't start, probably DMR problem

title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Tested in window mode, had to manually edit render.ini to get the desired resolution
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: The game runs with the latest Proton version. It did not detect my monitor's resolution correctly instead setting some weird 1264x634 resolution, I edited the game's config file (render.cfg) in "steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War" to get around this issue. The performance is not great, especially at higher resolutions.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Game runs perfect after the following tweaks. As it does not seem to be able to recognize the monitor resolution I had to define it with launch options "-w 2560 -h 1440 -fullscreen", without quotation marks.  Ones inside the game it still uses the wrong resolution until i change to "borderless" in the game display options. Then it uses the correct resolution. The change to borderless must be done each time you enter the game anew.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: The game does not recognize my monitors resolution. And in the in game settings menu it did not let me adjust the resolution for some reason. I therefore use the following launch options without quotations "-w 2560 -h 1440 -fullscreen". Ones inside the game, change the display settings from fullscreen to borderless, then the right resolution is used. This must be done every time you launch the game. Other than that the game runs perfectly.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Played with Proton 3.16-6 Beta, works out of the box now! Unable to get graphics improving, looks like the lowest settings but FPS runs at 120.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Fails with error message "Installed video device does not support DirectX 11 features."
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Had to install new AMD drivers that supported Vulkan to get the game working. After that it worked fine for the most part.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Resolution was weird, so it was needed to edit the file "render.cfg" in "steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War" and change the fields "ScreenHeight16by9" and "ScreenWidth16by9". After this tweak, everything ran just as expected.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: I had to fix the resolution in the render.cfg file, then perfect
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Game runs at playable frame rates (35-50fps) on Very High settings. As mentioned in other reports, some tweaks are needed to get the correct resolution.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes:
Had problems with the game crashing after 5-15 mins of play. Applying the same fix as described in the below reddit post for fixing similar issues with GTA V fixed it for me.
Now, unlike GTA V, the game still does crash on the rare occasion - on longer sessions. But I've had stable sessions with no problems over 5 h in length.
https://www.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/99ikj4/gta_v_is_running_pretty_well_for_me_in_proton/e5d5191/

Note that game updates can clear the steam app launch options value which can cause confusion.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: after changing "ScreenHeight16by9" and ScreenWidth16by9" in steamapps/compatdata/356190/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/WB Games/Shadow of War/render.cfg, it runs perfectly.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Change resolution in the render.cfg file to the proper width and height for your system and the game runs flawlessly afterwards. 45-60 fps on Ultra 1080p with a GTX 1070.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Runs 40 - 50 fps at high settings after correcting the resolutions in render.cfg.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™

notes: Runs perfectly except few hangs loading video sequences, but very rarely. 4x3 ratio screen (1600x1200) perfectly handled.
title: Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™
Middle-earth™: Shadow of War™
notes:
Can't connect to the internet on launch, so it launches a web browser with a code copy mechanism to verify ownership.

After this process, game crashes with the following error message.

"The Windows Local Application Data Folder is inaccessible. Access to this folder is required.

*USERDIR*\WB Games\Shadow of War\"

Manually creating this folder does not resolve the issue.
